Enum Class FireMode

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<FireMode>
lucis.lux.hff.enums.FireMode
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<FireMode>, Constable
public enum FireMode extends Enum<FireMode>
The FireMode enum defines the different firing modes available for firearms. Each mode specifies how a firearm behaves when the trigger is pulled, ranging from single shots to fully automatic fire. This classification is essential for gameplay mechanics, weapon balancing, and realistic simulation of firearms.

Fire modes influence the rate of fire, ammunition consumption, and tactical use of a weapon. For example, automatic weapons are suitable for suppressing fire, while single-action or bolt-action weapons are often used for precision shooting.

The supported fire modes are:

  • SELECT_FIRE: Allows the user to switch between different firing modes (e.g., semi-automatic and automatic).
  • SEMI_AUTOMATIC: Fires one round per trigger pull, automatically chambering the nect round.
  • BURST: Fires a fixed number of rounds (usually 2 or 3) per trigger pull.
  • AUTOMATIC: Continuously fires rounds as long the trigger is held down.
  • SINGLE_SHOT: Fires one round per trigger pull, requiring manual action to chamber the next round (e.g., bolt-action rifles).
  • SINGLE_ACTION: Requires the hammer to be manually cocked before each shot (e.g., single-action revolvers).
  • DOUBLE_ACTION: Cocks and fires the weapon with a single trigger pull (e.g., double-action revolvers).
  • MANUAL: Requires manual operation for each shot (e.g., muskets or break-action shotguns).
  • OTHER: Fire modes that do not fit into the above categories, such as experimental or custom designs.

  • Enum Constant Summary

    Enum Constants
    Enum Constant
    Description
    AUTOMATIC
    Continuously fires rounds as long as the trigger is held down.
    BURST
    Fires a fixed number of rounds (usually 2 or 3) per trigger pull.
    DOUBLE_ACTION
    Cocks and fires the weapon with a single trigger pull (e.g., double-action revolvers).
    MANUAL
    Requires manual operation for each shot (e.g., muskets or break-action shotguns).
    OTHER
    Fire modes that do not fit into the above categories, such as experimental or custom designs.
    SELECT_FIRE
    Allows switching between different firing modes (e.g., semi-automatic and automatic).
    SEMI_AUTOMATIC
    Fires one round per trigger pull, automatically chambering the next round.
    SINGLE_ACTION
    Requires the hammer to be manually cocked before each shot (e.g., single-action revolvers).
    SINGLE_SHOT
    Fires one round per trigger pull, requiring manual action to chamber the next round (e.g., bolt-action rifles).

    • SELECT_FIRE

      public static final FireMode SELECT_FIRE

      public static final FireMode SEMI_AUTOMATIC
      Fires one round per trigger pull, automatically chambering the next round.

    • BURST

      public static final FireMode BURST
      Fires a fixed number of rounds (usually 2 or 3) per trigger pull.

    • AUTOMATIC

      public static final FireMode AUTOMATIC
      Continuously fires rounds as long as the trigger is held down.

    • SINGLE_SHOT

      public static final FireMode SINGLE_SHOT
      Fires one round per trigger pull, requiring manual action to chamber the next round (e.g., bolt-action rifles).

    • SINGLE_ACTION

      public static final FireMode SINGLE_ACTION
      Requires the hammer to be manually cocked before each shot (e.g., single-action revolvers).

    • DOUBLE_ACTION

      public static final FireMode DOUBLE_ACTION
      Cocks and fires the weapon with a single trigger pull (e.g., double-action revolvers).

    • MANUAL

      public static final FireMode MANUAL
      Requires manual operation for each shot (e.g., muskets or break-action shotguns).

    • OTHER

      public static final FireMode OTHER
      Fire modes that do not fit into the above categories, such as experimental or custom designs.

  • Method Details

    • values

      public static FireMode[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.
      Returns:
      an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared

    • valueOf

      public static FireMode valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this class. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
      Returns:
      the enum constant with the specified name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this enum class has no constant with the specified name
      NullPointerException - if the argument is null
